description: Authentic sun-dried Nethili (Anchovies) sourced directly from coastal fishermen. Traditionally processed with optimal salt and sun-dried under hygienic conditions. Rich in protein, Omega-3 fatty acids, and calcium.
howToUse: Wash thoroughly in warm water 2-3 times before cooking. Best suited for spicy karuvadu gravy (Kuzhambu) or crispy shallow fry.
storageTips: Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place. Refrigiration extends shelf life up to 6 months.

reviews.title: Excellent Quality
reviews.comment: Very clean and perfectly salted. Tastes just like the ones we get directly from the coast.

reviews.title: Very Tasty
reviews.comment: Good packaging. No smell leakage. The fish fry turned out extremely crispy.

description: Premium quality Sura Karuvadu (Dried Shark Meat Pieces). Known for its firm texture and rich coastal taste. Sun-dried with minimal moisture, ideal for traditional medicinal and culinary uses.
howToUse: Soak in hot water for 10 minutes to remove excess salt, shred into small pieces, and cook. Perfect for Sura Putu (scrambled shark curry).
storageTips: Store in a dry airtight jar. Avoid moisture contact.

reviews.title: Perfect for Sura Putu
reviews.comment: Clean bone-free pieces. Putu came out wonderful, highly recommend this.

description: Delicious sun-dried coastal squid (Kanava). Sun-dried whole squid that captures the absolute essence of coastal seafood. Highly nutritious and low in fat.
howToUse: Soak in hot water for 15 minutes, clean the inner skin if any, and cut into rings. Best prepared as a dry spicy masala fry.
storageTips: Keep in refrigerator for longer preservation.

description: Thick-cut, premium sun-dried Paarai Karuvadu. Freshly caught Kingfish sliced and naturally sun-dried by coastal fishermen. Has a rich, meaty flavor and soft texture when cooked.
howToUse: Soak in water for 5 minutes, rinse, and shallow fry with chili powder and turmeric, or add to tangy tamarind-based fish curries.
storageTips: Keep airtight. Refrigerate for up to 90 days.

description: Traditional coastal sun-dried Catfish (Kelanga). Cleaned and salted to perfection, sun-dried naturally on coastal sandbeds using clean raised platforms.
howToUse: Wash in warm water and fry with garlic and onions. Complements simple curd rice or rasam rice beautifully.
storageTips: Store in dry place away from sunlight.

description: Extremely premium Koduva (Sea Bass) sun-dried steaks. Thick, flesh-rich slices containing very few bones, dry-cured naturally. Highly sought after for its rich coastal taste.
howToUse: Desalt slightly, then fry with mustard, red chilies, and curry leaves. Also tastes excellent in dry-fish gravy.

description: Homestyle Nethili Karuvadu Thokku (Dry Fish Pickle) prepared with cold-pressed sesame oil, homemade spices, garlic, and tamarind. Naturally preserved without artificial additives.
howToUse: Ready to eat! Serve as a side dish for white rice, idli, dosa, or chapati.
storageTips: Always use a clean dry spoon. Keep in a dry cupboard or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

reviews.title: Stunning Taste!
reviews.comment: Spicy, tangy, and filled with plenty of dry fish pieces. The sesame oil aroma is wonderful.

description: Rich and spicy Sura Karuvadu Thokku (Shark Pickle) cooked slowly with premium quality sun-dried shark shreds and authentic chettinad spices. Packaged hygienically to seal in freshness.
howToUse: Directly serve with hot steamed rice or curd rice.
storageTips: Store in a cool dry place.

description: An ultimate curation of our best sellers. Includes Nethili Karuvadu (100g), Sura Karuvadu (100g), Paarai Karuvadu (250g), and Nethili Karuvadu Thokku (250g glass jar). Perfect gift for dry fish lovers.
howToUse: See individual packaging guidelines for cooking methods.

description: Premium sun-dried prawns (Iral Vathal), small and firm with a concentrated, naturally salty-sweet flavor. A coastal Tamil Nadu staple, distinct from karuvadu, used in chutneys, kuzhambu, and stir-fries.
howToUse: Dry roast briefly or soak for 5 minutes to soften. Excellent in gongura chutney, sambar, or a simple stir-fry with shallots and curry leaves.
storageTips: Store in an airtight container away from moisture. Refrigerate for extended freshness.

description: A traditional spice blend made for dry fish kuzhambu and curries — chili, coriander, fennel, and roasted spices ground fresh. Cuts prep time without compromising on authentic coastal flavor.
howToUse: Add 1-2 tablespoons while preparing karuvadu kuzhambu or thokku, adjusting to taste. Also works well in prawn or regular fish curries.
storageTips: Keep in a dry airtight container, away from direct sunlight. Best used within 6 months for peak aroma.
